title: rosa urbica f. genuina
intro: Rosa urbica f. genuina, also known as the wild rose, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The health benefits of Rosa urbica f. genuina include its ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Rosa urbica f. genuina, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the flowers in hot water, while its leaves and stems can be used to make a medicinal oil or salve.
